title: AIAA-2006-154-960
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 2]
1.  Mathematical equations:

    d(rho)/dt = d(rhos*tilde(uj))/dxj

    d(rho*ui)/dt = d/dxj * [rho*tilde(ui)*tilde(uj) + p*deltaij + tau_ij + tau_ij^sgs]

    d(rho*E)/dt = d/dxj * [(rho*tilde(E) +tilde( p))*tilde(ui) + qi + U_j*tau_ij + H_j^sgs + sigma_ij^sgs]

     d(rho*Yk)/dt = Ss,k - d/dxj *[rho*tilde(Yk)*tilde(ui) + rho*tilde(Vk,i,k)+ Y_i,k^sgs + theta_i,k^sgs] + wk

2. NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

3. NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

4. NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

   * 3 mm - distance downstream of the flare exit where a counter-rotating feature was observed
   * 90 degrees - the sector volume used in the configuration
   * 20-35 atmospheres - realistic operating conditions

title: AIAA-2006-154-960
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Okay, here's the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   τ<sub>ij</sub> = μ(∂ũ<sub>i</sub>/∂x<sub>j</sub> + ∂ũ<sub>j</sub>/∂x<sub>i</sub> - 2/3 (∂ũ<sub>k</sub>/∂x<sub>k</sub>)δ<sub>ij</sub>)
*   q̃<sub>i</sub>= - κ̃ ∂T/∂x<sub>i</sub> + ρ Σ<sub>k=1</sub><sup>Ns</sup> h<sub>k</sub> Ỹ<sub>k</sub> Ṽ<sub>i,k</sub> + Σ<sub>k=1</sub><sup>Ns</sup> q̃<sub>i,k</sub><sup>sgs</sup>
*   Ṽ<sub>i,k</sub>= (-D<sub>k</sub>/Ỹ<sub>k</sub>)(∂Ỹ<sub>k</sub>/∂x<sub>i</sub>)
*   κ̃ = μC<sub>p</sub>/Pr
*   p̃ = ρR<sub>u</sub> Σ<sub>k=1</sub><sup>Ns</sup> (Ỹ<sub>k</sub>T̃/MW<sub>k</sub> + T̃<sub>k</sub><sup>sgs</sup>/MW<sub>k</sub>)
*   T̃<sub>k</sub><sup>sgs</sup>= [Ỹ<sub>k</sub>T̃ - Ỹ<sub>k</sub>T̃]
*   p̃Ẽ = p̃ẽ + 1/2 p̃ũ<sub>i</sub>ũ<sub>i</sub> + p̃k<sup>sgs</sup>
*   k<sup>sgs</sup>= (1/2)[ũ<sub>k</sub>ũ<sub>k</sub> - ũ<sub>k</sub>ũ<sub>k</sub>]
*   ẽ = Σ<sub>k=1</sub><sup>Ns</sup>[Ỹ<sub>k</sub>h̃<sub>k</sub> + (Ỹ<sub>k</sub>h<sub>k</sub> - Ỹ<sub>k</sub>h̃<sub>k</sub>)] - p̃/ρ
*   h̃<sub>k</sub>= Δh<sup>0</sup><sub>f,k</sub>+ ∫<sup>T̃</sup><sub>T<sup>0</sup></sub> C<sub>p,k</sub>(T')dT'
*   ẽ<sup>sgs</sup> = Σ<sub>k=1</sub><sup>Ns</sup>[c<sub>v,k</sub>Ỹ<sub>k</sub>T̃ + c<sub>v,k</sub>T̃<sup>sgs</sup> + Ỹ<sub>k</sub>Δh<sup>0</sup><sub>f,k</sub>]
*   Δh̃<sub>k</sub>= Δh<sup>0</sup><sub>f,k</sub>- c<sub>p,k</sub>T<sup>0</sup>
*   τ̃<sub>ij</sub><sup>sgs</sup>=  ρ̃(ũ<sub>i</sub>ũ<sub>j</sub> - ũ<sub>i</sub>ũ<sub>j</sub>)
*   H̃ <sup>sgs</sup>  = ρ̃(Ẽũ<sub>i</sub> - Ẽũ<sub>i</sub>) + (p̃ũ<sub>i</sub> - p̃ũ<sub>i</sub>)
*   σ̃<sub>i</sub><sup>sgs</sup>=  ũ<sub>j</sub>τ̃<sub>ij</sub> - ũ<sub>j</sub>τ̃<sub>ij</sub>
*   Ỹ<sub>i,k</sub><sup>sgs</sup>=  ρ̃[ũ<sub>i</sub>Ỹ<sub>k</sub> - ũ<sub>i</sub>Ỹ<sub>k</sub>]
*   q̃<sub>i,k</sub><sup>sgs</sup> =  [h̃<sub>k</sub>D<sub>k</sub>∂Ỹ<sub>k</sub>∂x<sub>i</sub> - h̃<sub>k</sub>D<sub>k</sub>∂Ỹ<sub>k</sub>/∂x<sub>i</sub>]
*   θ̃<sub>i,k</sub><sup>sgs</sup>=  ρ[Ṽ<sub>i,k</sub>Ỹ<sub>k</sub> - Ṽ<sub>i,k</sub>Ỹ<sub>k</sub>

title: AIAA-2006-154-960
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's the extracted technical content:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   ∂/∂t (ρksgs) + ∂/∂xᵢ (ρuᵢksgs) = Psgs - Dsgs + ∂/∂xᵢ ( (νt/σk) ∂ksgs/∂xᵢ ) + Fd
*   Psgs = -τᵢⱼsgs (∂ũᵢ/∂xⱼ)
*   Dsgs = Cερ(ksgs)3/2/Δ
*   Fd = ũᵢFₛ,ᵢ - ũᵢFₛ,ᵢ
*   τᵢⱼsgs = CLLᵢⱼ
*   ktest = 1/2 [ρuₖũₖ² - (ρũₖ)² / ρ]
*   τᵢⱼsgs / C̄L = Lᵢⱼ = 1/C̄L [- 2ρCᵥ √ktest Δ̂(<Sᵢⱼ> - 1/3 <Sₖₖ>δᵢⱼ) + 2/3 ρ ktest δᵢⱼ]
*   Cᵥ = L'ᵢⱼ Mᵢⱼ / 2MᵢⱼMᵢⱼ
*   L'ᵢⱼ = Lᵢⱼ - 2/3 ρ ktestδᵢⱼ
*   Mᵢⱼ = ρ̂ √ktest Δ̂(<Sᵢⱼ> - 1/3 <Sₖₖ>δᵢⱼ)
*   Ce = Δ̂(μ + μt) / ρ̂ ktest³⁄₂ [ ( Tᵢⱼ ∂ũⱼ / ∂xᵢ) - Tᵢⱼ ∂ũⱼ / ∂xᵢ ]
*   μt = νt * ρ
*   Hᵢsgs = -ρ νt/Prt ∂H/∂xᵢ

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, & Parameters:**

*   σk = model constant typically equal to unity
*   Cᵥ, Cε = Coefficients obtained dynamically
*   Nominal "constant" values for the coefficients: 0.067 and 0.916
*   Δ (or Δ̂) = grid filter level
*   μ = molecular viscosity
*   νt = eddy viscosity

title: AIAA-2006-154-960
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 5]
1.  Mathematical equations:
    *   H = h + (u_i * u_i)/2 + k_sgs, where h = sum_{k=1}^{N_s} h_k Y_k
    *   Y_i,k^{sgs} = (rho * nu_t / Sc_t) * (d(Y_k) / dx_i)
    *   rho * (d(Y_k) / dt) = -rho * [u_i + (u_i)^R + (u_i)^S] * (d(Y_k) / dx_i) - (d/dx_i) * (rho * Y_k * V_i,k) + w_k + S_s,k
    *   (Y_k^{n+1} - Y_k^n) / (delta t_LES) = -[u_i + (u_i)^R] * (d(Y_k)^n/dx_i)
    * Y_k^{n+1} - Y_k^*= int_t^(t+delta t_LES) { 1/rho * [ -rho * (u_i)^S * (d(Y_k)/dx_i) + (d/dx_i (rho * Y_k * V_i,k)^n) - w_k^n - S_s,k^n ] } dt

2.  Graphs/charts: NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

3.  Tables: NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

4.  Diagrams: NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

5.  Key numbers, constants, parameters:
    *   Pr_t is a turbulent Prandtl number, assumed to be unity.
    *   Sc_t is the turbulent Schmidt Number, assumed to be unity
    *   delta t_LES is the LES time-step

title: AIAA-2006-154-960
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here's the extracted technical content:

1.  **Mathematical Equations:**

*   dxi.p / dt = ui.p
*   dui.p / dt = (f / tau_V) * (ui - ui.p) + gi
*   f = (CD * Red) / 24
*   tau_V = (rho_p * dp^2) / (18 * mu_g)
*   CD =

    *   (24/Red) * (1 + (1/6)*Red)^(2/3), Red <= 1000
    *   0.4392, Red > 1000
*   dmp / dt = - m_dot_p
*   m_dot_p / m_dot_p,Red=0 = 1 + (0.278 * sqrt(Red) * Sc^(1/3)) / ( [1 + (1.232/ sqrt(Red) * Sc^(1/3))^(1/2)] )
*   m_dot_p,Red=0 = 2 * pi * rho_s * Dsm * dp * ln(1 + BM)
*   BM = b_infinity - b_s =  (YF,s - YF,infinity) / (1-YF,s), where b = YF / (YF -1)

2.  **Graphs/charts:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

3.  **Tables:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

4.  **Diagrams:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

5.  **Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   dp: parcel diameter
*   rp: parcel radius
*   rho_p: liquid density
*   mu_g: gas-phase dynamic viscosity
*   vg: gas-phase kinematic viscosity
*   CD: drag coefficient
*   Red: relative particle Reynolds number
*   ui.p: i-th component of parcel velocity
*   ui: i-th component of gas-phase velocity
*   xi.p: i-th component of parcel position
*   f: drag factor
*   tau_V: particle velocity response time
*   gi: i-th gravity component
*   m_dot_p: net mass transfer rate
*   Dp: mixture diffusion coefficient at the droplet surface
*   BM: Spalding transfer number
*   YF: fuel species mass fraction
*   Sc: Schmidt number
* rho_s: the gas mixture density

title: AIAA-2006-154-960
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 7]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   `mp*Cl*dTp/dt = Qconv -  mp*Lv = hp*pi*dp^2*(T - Tp) - mp*Lv`  (Equation 23)
*   `((P_s~),(F_s,i~),(Q_s~),(S_s,k~)) = 1/deltaV * (( Sum_m n_p[m_p.], sum_m n_p[m_p*ui,p - 4/3*rho_p*r_p^3*du/dt], sum_m n_p[m_p*h_vs - hp*pi*dp^2*(T - Tp) - ui,p*mp*du/dt + mp*(1/2*ui,p^2)], sum_m n_p[m_p.])`
* `w_k = Wk * sum_j=1^Nr (v'_k,j - v''_k,j) * min(q_f,j^mix, q_f,j^kin)`
*   `q_j,f^kin = k_f,j * PROD_1^Ns [M_k]^v'_k,j – k_b,j * PROD_1^Ns [M_k]^v''_k,j
*   `q_j,f^mix = 1/tau_M  min([M_k]/v'_k,j)`
*   `q_j,b^mix = 1/tau_M  min([M_k]/v''_k,j)`   (Equation 24)
*   `tau_M = C_EBU * delta/k^0.5`

**2. Graphs/Charts:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, & Parameters:**

*   `mp`: particle mass
*   `Cl`: liquid heat capacity
*   `hp`: heat transfer coefficient
*   `Lv`: latent heat of vaporization
*   `np`: number of particles present per parcel/group
*   `deltaV`: volume of computational cell
*   `C12H23`: liquid fuel (approximating Jet-A)
*   `CEBU = 1`
*   `tau_M`: mixing time scale.

**Chemical Reactions:**

*   `C12H23 + (35/2)O2 -> 12CO + (23/2)H2O`
*   `2CO + O2 -> 2CO2`
*   `N2 + O2 -> 2NO`

*   Total mass (air) flow rate: 30.5 g/s
*   Fuel mass flow rate: 0.750 g/s
*   Overall equivalence ratio: 0.40
*   Pre-heat air temperature: 379K
*   Combustor grid dimensions: (185x21x21) and (185x74x81)
*   Combustor length: 0.278 m
*   Combustor height: 0.0896 m
*   Combustor width: 0.0737 m (lower), 0.103 m (upper)
*   Circular inlet diameter (Do): 0.04026 m
*   Inlet location: approximately 0.77Ro upstream of dump plane
*   Bulk flow velocity (U0): 45.9 m/s
*   Bluff-body radius: 0.19Ro, origin at r/Ro = 0.0

*   Reynolds number (ReD): 76,212
*   Turbulence Reynolds number (Ret) :  9,800 (non-reacting) & 17,446 (reacting),
*   Subgrid Reynolds number (Re{\Delta}): 113 and 187
*   Kolmogorov scale (\eta): 0.009 mm and 0.006 mm
*   Sauter-Mean Diameter (SMD): 31.2 micron
*   Droplet cutoff radius: 4 µm
*   Particle injection distance: 1 mm
*   Minimum axial resolution: 0.3 mm
*   Minimum radial resolution: 0.26 mm
*   Minimum azimuthal resolution: 0.25 mm

title: AIAA-2006-154-960
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 15]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided page:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   There are no equations written in symbolic form.  The plots show slopes labeled as "-(5/3)".

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 2(a): Inflow Profiles**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: r/R0 (dimensionless radial position)
        *   Y-axis: U/U0 (dimensionless velocity)
    *   Curves:
        *   Ux (axial velocity): Starts near 0, increases, then rapidly decreases around r/R0  = 0.4, then gradually increases.
        *   Ur (radial velocity): Stays near 0 for the range of r/R0.
        *   Uθ (tangential velocity): Starts at ~1.25, decreases to ~0.2 at r/R0 = .4, slowly increases.

*   **Figure 2(b): Particle Size Distribution**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: Particle Radius (R), meter (units seem incorrect, likely should be micrometers instead of meters)
        *   Y-axis: fR(R)
    *   Curves::
        *   Data points show a curve with a rapid decrease in fR(R) as particle radius increases.  Around R = 16µm shows slope roughly equal to 1.

*   **Figure 3: Kinetic Energy Spectrum**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: Frequency (Hz) [log scale]
        *   Y-axis: Kinetic Energy (m2/s2)  [log scale]
    *   Curves:
        *   Reacting (solid, red): Shows a high-energy state, and has a decay with a slope "-(5/3)" in the inertial subrange.
        *   Non-reacting (dashed, blue): Lower in its energy and decays close to that of the reacting curve.
    * Data:
        * Probe location: x/Ro = 0.0, z/Ro = 0.308

**3. Tables:**

*     NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   SMD (Sauter Mean Diameter) = 31.2 µm
*   Slope in kinetic energy spectra = -(5/3)

I have tried to keep the output concise while including all the relevant data.  I also corrected the Particle Radius (R) units.

title: AIAA-2006-154-960
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 21]
Here's the breakdown of the technical content according to your request:

**Graphs/Charts**

*   **Figure (b) Reacting X - Y Plane:**  Shows mean axial velocity contours in the horizontal plane.
    *   Ordinate axis labeled  "A" through "J". The value associated to the letters are: A(-11), B(-8), C(-2), D(1) ,E(5), F(11),G(17), H(22), I(34), J(40)
    *   Different colour shaded regions represent velocity contours.

*   **Figure (c) Fuel Reaction Rate:** Shows time-averaged fuel reaction rate.
    *   Ordinade axis labeled  "A" through "F". The value associated to the letters are: A(-10), B(10), C(20), D(30), E(40)
    *   Ordinate axis (left): Values represented by colours range between -5E-05, -2E-05, 0E+00.

*   **Figure (d) Mixing and Chemical Rate:** Shows mixing and chemical rate contours.
        *   Ordinate axis labeled  "A" through "F". The value associated to the letters are: A(0.0E+00), B(4,0E-05), C(8.0E-05), D(1.2E-04), E(1.6E-04), F(2.0E-04)
    *   Values represented by color gradients range between 0E+00, 1E-05, 3E-05.

**Diagrams**

*   **Figure (a) Reacting VBB:**  Shows an axial velocity iso-surface at zero velocity. It also shows streamlines from the EBULES simulation.

**Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters**

*   Reaction rates are in CGS (centimeter-gram-second) units.

**Other**

*   EBULES Simulation
